title: citrus × aurantium subf. tengu
intro: Citrus × aurantium subf. tengu, also known as bitter orange or Seville orange, is a hybrid of sour orange (Citrus × aurantium) and sweet orange (Citrus sinensis). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of nausea and headaches. The health benefits of Citrus × aurantium subf. tengu include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve circulation, and boost the immune system, as well as its use in weight loss and appetite suppression. Herbal preparations of Citrus × aurantium subf. tengu can be found in teas, tinctures, and capsules, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
